📖 标准知识库 🎯 我方策略 🏢 认证执行

📖 EMT 标准知识库

ANCE NMX-J-536 ♦ CSA C22.2 No.83.1-07 ♦ UL 797 — 2023-04-28 协调版 · 转录源 UL 797-2023。三大模块：① 快速定位（按功能逐项）② 关于要求（逐条 + 数据表 + 图清单 + 校验）③ 标准全景（背景分析，可折叠）。点任意 🔍 看源页扫描截图，模态同时给「印刷页 / UL PDF 页码」并可跳转 PDF。

📍 按功能快速定位 — 11 类逐项要求

逐项要求按功能归类，附条款号与 UL 797-2023 PDF 阅读器页码（点 🔍 看原页扫描）。标准未规定 = 标准明确没有该项要求。

要求条款源页性质
🧬 1｜材料与化学成分
管体必须为钢制（steel）§5.1.1强制
未规定具体钢号，也未规定 C/Mn/P/S/Cr/Mo 化学成分限值§5.1.1标准未规定
未规定钢材最低抗拉/屈服/延伸率/硬度/冲击值标准未规定
↳ 钢号、化学成分、力学性能须由企业原材料采购规范另行控制
⚙️ 2｜制造工艺与焊缝
管体平直、圆形截面，所有接缝充分焊合§5.1.1强制
未指定必须用 ERW/高频焊/热镀锌等特定工艺标准未规定
焊缝内外无金属修边残留、锐边、尖锐凸起§5.1.2强制
内焊筋高度：1/2–2″ ≤0.38mm；2-1/2–6″ ≤0.51mm§5.1.2强制
涂覆前内外表面无氧化皮、飞边及其他凸起§5.1.3强制
管端切割方正，无毛刺和锐边§5.1.4强制
📐 3｜尺寸 · 长度 · 重量
规格 Trade size 1/2–6″（Table 5.1）§5.2.1强制
外径公差 ±0.005/±0.010/±0.015/±0.020 in（按规格递增）§5.2.1强制
最低单位重量 ≥ Table 5.1 的 kg/m 或 lbs/ft§5.2.1强制
标准长度 3.05 m(10 ft) ±6 mm(±1/4 in)§5.2.1强制
非标准长度：最低重量按 Table 5.1 与实际长度成比例§5.2.2强制
↳ 壁厚/内径在 Annex B，标注「For information only」
直线度：要求平直，但未给「10 ft 内 ≤0.1 in」数值公差§5.1.1标准未规定
🔧 4｜机械性能 · 弯曲试验
普通钢制 EMT 无最低抗拉/屈服/抗压/硬度门槛标准未规定
常温弯曲：弯 1/4 圆 — 金属不裂、焊缝不开、截面无明显变形§6.1.1强制
低温弯曲：0°C/60min；15 s 内开始；不裂、焊缝不开§6.1.2强制
涂层常温弯曲：弯后涂层无可见开裂剥落§6.2.1.1强制
涂层低温弯曲：弯后涂层不露裸基材、不与金属分离§6.2.1.3强制
非金属替代涂层冷冲击：0°C/60min，12.2 J(9 ft-lb)（仅非金属替代涂层）§6.2.4.10强制
🛡️ 5｜镀层 · 防腐体系
外壁：纯锌主防腐层，或经评估的替代防腐涂层§5.3.1.1强制
内壁：锌层 或 有机涂层§5.3.1.1强制
锌层：完全覆盖、牢固附着、平滑、无起泡及降低防腐能力的缺陷§5.3.2.1强制
未规定必须热浸镀锌，也未指定连续/预镀/成管后镀工艺标准未规定
未规定最低 μm/g·m⁻²/oz·ft⁻²，也无试验前后厚度差要求标准未规定
↳ 表面处理膜 ≤0.0038mm 可不按替代涂层完整评价（非锌层最低厚度）
🔬 6｜锌层硫酸铜 / Preece 试验
试样：成品镀锌管约 150 mm(6 in)§6.2.2.2强制
浸泡条件：硫酸铜溶液 18.3±1.1°C，每次 60 s§6.2.2.1强制
外壁锌层：4×60 s 后无明亮且牢固附着的铜沉积§6.2.2.8强制
内壁锌层：1×60 s 后无明亮且牢固附着的铜沉积§6.2.2.9强制
↳ 性质：锌层连续性/薄弱点性能试验，非直接测厚
🧪 7｜有机内涂层测试
外观：均匀、质量一致、表面平滑§5.3.4.1强制
弹性：试片 10 次连续 180° 往复弯曲后不裂/剥/损§6.2.3.1强制
温湿空气：60±1°C、98±2% RH、60 天后金属不腐蚀§6.2.3.2强制
🧫 8｜替代防腐涂层测试（PVC 等）
涂层弯曲 / 低温弯曲§6.2.1.1强制
非金属成分识别：IR、TGA、DSC§6.2.1.4强制
高温烘箱预处理：100±1°C、240 h§6.2.4.4强制
紫外光+水：碳弧 360 h 或 氙弧 500 h§6.2.4.5强制
盐雾：600 h§6.2.4.6强制
湿态 CO₂-SO₂-空气：1200 h§6.2.4.7强制
PVC 涂层抗拉 ≥13.79 MPa(2000 psi)（涂层性能，非钢管）§6.2.4.8强制
涂层附着力§6.2.4.9强制
冷冲击：12.2 J(9 ft-lb)§6.2.4.10强制
燃烧：3×60 s 施焰，每次续燃 ≤5 s；不点燃棉花、涂层不烧尽§6.2.4.11强制
↳ 替代涂层完整测试总览 → Annex C
🔌 9｜电气连续性 · 接头 · 附件
非金属替代涂层产品：配紧定螺钉式+压紧式接头做装配/弯曲/电阻/拉力/故障电流测试§5.3.3.2强制
补充涂层须评估：主防腐层/接头配合/电气连续性/非金属火焰传播影响§5.3.5.2强制
扩口整体接头/配件须符合相应导管及电缆配件标准§5.5.1强制
↪️ 10｜弯头要求
弯头用与直管相同等级管材，按相同要求处理/涂覆/生产§5.4.1强制
弯曲部分连续、平滑，不得折皱§5.4.3强制
最小弯曲半径 R 及端部直段 L₅ → Table 5.2Table 5.2强制
🏷️ 11｜成品检验 · 标识
成品外观：涂层连续；无裸点/氧化皮/毛刺/焊瘤/凹痕；焊缝与弯头无损Annex D强制
标识：制造商·商标 / 「EMT」/ 安装提示 / 替代涂层温度额定 / 补充涂层说明 / 制造日期§7强制
✅ 核心结论（7 条）
  1. UL 797 / CSA C22.2 No.83.1 不规定具体钢号和化学成分。
  2. 普通钢制 EMT 无最低抗拉/屈服/硬度要求；机械性能由常温+低温弯曲验证。
  3. 未规定必须用 ERW / 热浸镀锌 / 其他指定工艺。
  4. 未规定普通锌层最低厚度或镀锌量，也无试验前后厚度变化要求。
  5. 普通锌层核心防腐判定 = 硫酸铜/Preece：外壁 4×60 s，内壁 1×60 s 📖 内外壁分组图解 ↓。
  6. 非纯锌（PVC/替代涂层）须加 UV、水、盐雾、湿 CO₂-SO₂、附着、抗拉、冷冲击、燃烧等测试。
  7. EMT 合规非只看试验，还须同时满足材料类别/焊缝/尺寸/最低重量/涂层结构/外观/标识。

🖼 试验流程图解（真实步骤图 · 点击展开）

关键测试的真实步骤流程图，点 🔍 看标准源页扫描。更多测试图解陆续补充。

🔬 硫酸铜 / Preece 试验图解 — 内外壁为何分两组做（EMT 认证最易失败项 · 点击展开）
🅰 A 组 · 外壁测试（4 循环）

用完整的约 150 mm(6 in) 管段（不剖开）。浸入硫酸铜溶液 60 s → 取出 → 流水冲洗 → 干布擦去松散铜 → 干燥 → 检查外表面。同一试样重复 4 个循环；4 次均无明亮牢固附着铜 → 外壁通过。

🅱 B 组 · 内壁测试（1 循环）

把另一管段沿长度方向纵向剖开，使内壁完全暴露。浸泡 60 s → 冲洗 → 检查内表面。只做 1 个循环即判定。

为何不同：外壁是主防腐面、暴露环境最严苛 → 判 4×；内壁防护要求较低 → 判 1×。溶液 18.3±1.1°C；判据：不得出现明亮且牢固附着的铜沉积（深色/黑色铜斑不算失败）；管端附近 13 mm(小规格)/25 mm(大规格) 及剖切边 3 mm 范围内不计入。条款 §6.2.2.1–6.2.2.10，印刷页 12–13 / UL PDF p16–17。

🔧 弯曲试验图解 — 常温(§6.1.1) + 低温(§6.1.2)（点击展开）

常温弯曲（§6.1.1）：最小规格试样常温弯成 1/4 圆（90°）→ 目视；金属不开裂、焊缝/接缝不张开、圆形截面无明显变形。  低温弯曲（§6.1.2）：0°C(32°F) 调温 60 min → 取出 15 s 内开始 → 弯 1/4 圆（中心线半径 ≥ Table 5.2）；管体不裂、焊缝不开。注：§6.1.1 未规定固定试样长度或最小弯曲半径。

📋 关于要求（逐条 · 表 · 图）

📋 全部要求（按维度分类）

📐 公差 / 尺寸 11 条
条款要求判据 / 限值性质源页
5.1.2Welded seam free of metal trimmings/sharp edges/projections; interior weld bead height limitNo metal trimmings, sharp edges, or sharp projections (interior or exterior). Slight interior bead allowed if not sharp and not exceeding 0.38 mm (0.015 in) for trade size 16-53 (1/2-2), or 0.51 mm (0.020 in) for trade size 63-155 (2-1/2-6)强制
5.2.1Outside diameter, minimum weight, standard length, and length tolerance of finished tubing; visual examinationOD and minimum weight per Table 5.1; standard length 3.05 m (10 ft) ±6 mm (±1/4 in); each length visually examined on exterior and interior for absence/presence of poor coatings, hard scale, burrs/fins, or other defects (see Annex D)强制
5.2.2Non-standard lengths weight and length toleranceNon-standard lengths shall have minimum acceptable weight proportional to Table 5.1 weights; length tolerance of 5.2.1 (±6 mm / ±1/4 in) applies to non-standard lengths强制
Table 5.1Outside diameter and minimum acceptable weight for each EMT trade size (metric designators 16–155 / trade sizes 1/2–6).OD with tolerance per size, e.g. 16: 17.93±0.13 mm (0.706±0.005 in); 21: 23.42±0.13 mm; 27: 29.54±0.13 mm; 35: 38.35±0.13 mm; 41: 44.20±0.13 mm; 53: 55.80±0.13 mm; 63: 73.03±0.25 mm (2.875±0.010 in); 78: 88.90±0.38 mm (3.500±0.015 in); 91: 101.60±0.50 mm (4.000±0.020 in); 103: 114.30±0.50 mm; 129: 141.30±0.50 mm; 155: 168.28±0.50 mm. OD tolerance tightens by size: ±0.13/±0.005in for ≤2in, ±0.25/±0.010in @2-1/2, ±0.38/±0.015in @3, ±0.50/±0.020in @3-1/2 and up. Min weight ranges 0.424–5.506 kg/m metric column; 0.285–8.37 lbs/ft imperial column.强制
5.2.3Other dimensions of EMT are provided in Annex B.For information only (Annex B reference); not a mandatory limit.参考
Table 5.2Minimum elbow dimensions: centerline radius R and straight-end length L₅ per trade size (metric 16–155 / trade 1/2–6).These are minimums (not symmetric tolerances). Radius R, mm: 16→102, 21→114, 27→146, 35→184, 41→210, 53→241, 63→267, 78→330, 91→381, 103→406, 129→610, 155→762 (in: 4, 4-1/2, 5-3/4, 7-1/4, 8-1/4, 9-1/2, 10-1/2, 13, 15, 16, 24, 30). L₅, mm: 16→38, 21→38, 27→48, 35→51, 41→51, 53→51, 63→76, 78→79, 91→83, 103→86, 129→92, 155→95 (in: 1-1/2, 1-1/2, 1-7/8, 2, 2, 2, 3, 3-1/8, 3-1/4, 3-3/8, 3-5/8, 3-3/4).强制
Figure 1EMT 90-degree elbow geometry (referenced by Clause 5.4.3): bend angle and defined bend radius R and outside diameter dBend angle 90°±2°; bend radius R from radius center to inner curve; diameter d at open ends; centerlines marked on both legs强制
6.2.4.11.2Flame-test fixture dimensions (Figure 4): distance from barrel-tip plane to point B, minimum distance to lower specimen support, and distance to top surface of cottonTip-plane to point B = 38 mm (1 1/2 in); 76 mm (3 in) MIN to lower support for specimen; 229–241 mm (9–9 1/2 in) to top surface of cotton; burner angle 20°强制
6.2.4.11.5Figure 5 wedge dimensions for the flame test (drawing SB1214C), dimensioned in mm (inches)Top edge 76 mm (3 in); left vertical 83 mm (3-1/4 in); to center of burner 38 mm (1-1/2 in); sloped right face 83 mm (3-1/4 in); right-edge thickness 25 mm (1 in); base length 159 mm (6-1/4 in); base slope angle 20°强制
B.1Dimensions of EMT-Steel: nominal internal diameter and wall thickness by metric designator / trade size (informative table, see Clause 5.2.3)12 sizes, metric designators 16-155 (trade 1/2 in to 6 in). ID 15.80-163.45 mm (0.622-6.435 in); US/Canada wall 1.07-2.41 mm (0.042-0.095 in); Mexico wall 1.1-2.4 mm. e.g. trade 1/2: ID 15.80 mm/0.622 in, wall 1.07 mm/0.042 in (US/CA), 1.1 mm (MX); trade 1 in: US/CA wall 0.057 in vs MX 0.060 in. Values for information only.参考
F.1 (Table F1)Master bundle quantity for 3.05-m (10-foot) lengths by trade sizePer-trade-size pieces/feet/meters and nominal weight, e.g. 1/2 (metric 16): 700 pcs / 7000 ft (2133.6 m) / 2100 lb (948.0 kg); 3/4 (21): 500/5000/1524/2300/1037.8; 1 (27): 300/3000/914.4/2010/916.3; 1-1/4 (35): 200/2000/609.6/2020/911.7; 1-1/2 (41): 150/1500/457.2/1740/789.2; 2 (53): 120/1200/365.8/1776/807.4; 2-1/2 (63): 61/610/185.9/1318/598.7; 3 (78): 51/510/155.4/1341/607.8; 3-1/2 (91): 37/370/112.8/1291/585.1; 4 (103): 30/300/91.4/1179/535.2; 5 (129): 25/250/76.2/1390/629.7; 6 (155): 20/200/61.0/1328/601.7参考
🔧 机械 5 条
条款要求判据 / 限值性质源页
5.1.4Tube end conditionEnd of tubing shall be cut off squarely and without any burrs and sharp edges强制
6.1.1Ambient-temperature bend test on smallest available trade size.At ambient temperature, one specimen of smallest available trade size shall bend into a quarter circle without cracks in metal, opening of seam/weld, or appreciable distortion of circular cross-section. Compliance for cracks/openings determined by visual examination after bending.强制
6.2.1.3Cold-bend test: one smallest-trade-size finished tube conditioned at 0°C (32°F) for 60 min shall bend into a quarter circle (centerline radius ≥ Table 5.2) without the tube cracking or the weld opening, and without coating damage exposing bare metal or causing coating separationCondition 0°C (32°F) for 60 min; bend = quarter circle, centerline radius ≥ Table 5.2; no crack, weld shall not open, no bare metal exposed, no coating separation; test done inside cold chamber or begun within 15 s of removal; nonmetallic alternate coating marked below 0°C conditioned at rated temperature (any temp below 0°C in 5°C/9°F increments)强制
6.2.3.1.1Organic interior coating elasticity: when applied to sheet-steel test piece and baked 5 h, shall withstand 10 successive 180° bends back and forth against a 1.6 mm radius edge without damageBaked 5 h; withstand 10 successive bends back and forth through 180° against edge radius 1.6 mm (1/16 in) without damage强制
6.2.4.8.1PVC alternate corrosion-resistant coatings shall meet minimum tensile strength; other materials subject to special investigationPVC: minimum tensile strength 13.79 MPa (2000 psi) when tested per Ref Item No. 16 Annex A; other materials subject to special investigation强制
🧪 化学 5 条
条款要求判据 / 限值性质源页
6.2.1.4Nonmetallic material used as alternate corrosion-resistant or organic coating shall undergo IR, TGA, and DSC tests per the applicable polymeric-materials standard for compound identification (Ref Item No. 7, Annex A)Subject to infrared spectroscopy (IR), thermogravimetric analysis (TGA), and differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) per applicable standard for polymeric materials强制
6.2.2.1Copper sulfate (Preece) test solution preparation: reagent-grade cupric sulfate (CuSO₄) dissolved in hot distilled water in a copper/glass/polyethylene/nonreactive container containing a bright copper piece; neutralize free acid; dilute and filter to target specific gravitySpecific gravity 1.183–1.189 at 18.3°C (65.0°F) after cooling; neutralize free acid with ~1 g cupric oxide (CuO) or ~1 g cupric hydroxide (Cu(OH)₂) per liter; dilute with distilled water to s.g. 1.183–1.189 at 18.3°C; then filter强制
6.2.4.7.3Gas charging and chamber humidity/support conditions for moist CO2-SO2-air testCO2 and SO2 supplied from commercial pressurized cylinders; CO2 equal to 1 percent of test chamber volume plus equal volume of SO2 introduced each working day; purge previous day's gas-air mixture before new charge; maintain small amount of water at chamber bottom for humidity (not changed during exposure); specimens supported in plastic racks at 15 degrees from vertical强制
A.1 item 4Polymeric materials property evaluation reference cited at clause 5.3.5.2US: UL 746A Polymeric Materials – Short Term Property Evaluations + UL 514B; Canada: CSA C22.2 No.0.17-00 (R2018) Evaluation of Properties of Polymeric Materials + No.18.3; Mexico: NMX-J-023/1-ANCE-2000 + NMX-J-017-ANCE强制
A.1 item 7Polymeric materials short-term property evaluation reference cited at clause 6.2.1.4US: UL 746A; Canada: CSA C22.2 No.0.17-00 (R2018); Mexico: NMX-J-023/1-ANCE-2000强制
🛡 镀层 28 条
条款要求判据 / 限值性质源页
1.1Scope: EMT exterior and interior coating provisionEMT provided with a zinc, zinc-based, nonmetallic, or other alternate corrosion-resistant exterior coating and an organic or zinc interior coating强制
3.2Definition: alternate corrosion-resistant coatingPrimary coating(s) other than solely zinc that demonstrates required corrosion resistance on exterior of tubing (see 6.2.4); may include zinc强制
3.3Definition: organic coatingInterior coating(s) other than solely zinc that demonstrates required corrosion resistance where not subject to physical damage (see 6.2.3); may include zinc强制
3.4Definition: zinc coatingPrimary coating consisting solely of zinc that demonstrates required corrosion resistance on exterior of tubing (see 6.2.2)强制
3.7Definition: primary coatingA coating on the outside of the tubing for protection against corrosion (see 5.3.1.1)强制
3.8Definition: supplementary coatingA coating on the outside of the tubing in addition to the primary coating, for protection against severe corrosive conditions (see 5.3.5)强制
5.3.1.1Exterior surface shall be protected against corrosion by a coating solely of zinc or an alternate corrosion-resistant coating; interior surface shall be protected by zinc or an organic coating.Exterior: zinc (per 5.3.2) OR alternate corrosion-resistant coating (per 5.3.3). Interior: zinc (5.3.2) OR organic coating (5.3.4). Overview in Annex C.强制
5.3.2.1Zinc protective coating quality and adhesion requirements.Shall cover completely; adhere firmly at all points; be smooth and free from blisters/defects that lessen protective value; be in metal-to-metal contact with the steel; and comply with Clause 6.2.2.强制
5.3.3.1Alternate corrosion-resistant coating quality and adhesion requirements.Shall cover completely; adhere firmly at all points; be smooth and free from blisters/other defects that lessen protective value.强制
5.3.4.1Organic coating quality requirements.Shall cover evenly the surface applied; be of uniform quality throughout; have a smooth and even appearance; and comply with Clause 6.2.3 and the bending requirements of Clause 6.2.1.1.强制
5.3.6.1Thin surface treatments exempt from alternate/organic coating requirements.Surface treatment(s) not exceeding 0.0038 mm (0.00015 in) used as top coat or conversion coating need not meet alternate corrosion-resistant coating or organic coating requirements.强制
6.2.1.1Protective coating (interior/exterior) shall not crack or flake when smallest available trade-size finished specimen is bent into a semi-circle at ambient temperature, using bending equipment producing an elbow centerline radius not less than Table 5.2No cracking or flaking visible to normal or corrected-to-normal vision; centerline bend radius ≥ Table 5.2 value; specimen = smallest available trade size; ambient temperature; bent into a semi-circle强制
6.2.2.8Exterior zinc coating that is sole primary corrosion resistance shall pass the copper sulfate test without bright adherent copper deposit after the specified immersionsNo bright, adherent copper deposit after four 60-s immersions in copper sulfate solution (exterior)强制
6.2.2.9Interior zinc coating that is sole primary corrosion resistance shall pass the copper sulfate test without bright adherent copper deposit after the specified immersionNo bright, adherent copper deposit after one 60-s immersion in copper sulfate solution (interior)强制
6.2.4.5.2Pass criteria for UV light and water exposure - corrosion/blistering and red rust creep limitsUnscribed: no base metal corrosion or blisters. Scribed: average red rust creep from scribe not greater than Rating No. 6 (1.6-3.2 mm / 1/16-1/8 in) per Ref Item No. 10 Annex A, max isolated spot not exceeding 9.5 mm (3/8 in); no visual pitting of substrate; only beginning of red rust buildup beneath coating uplifted from scribe强制
6.2.4.6.2Pass criteria for salt spray (fog) exposure - corrosion, pitting, red rust creep, and coating separation limitsUnscribed: no more than light corrosion beneath coating system, no visible pitting of substrate, only beginning of buildup/weeping of red rust. Scribed: average red rust creep not greater than Rating No. 5 (3.2-4.8 mm / 1/8-3/16 in) per Ref Item No. 14 Annex A, max isolated spot not exceeding 9.5 mm (3/8 in); no separation of coating from substrate强制
6.2.4.7.2Pass criteria for moist CO2-SO2-air exposure - corrosion, pitting, red rust creep, and coating separation limitsUnscribed: no more than light corrosion beneath coating system, no visible pitting of substrate, only beginning of buildup/weeping of red rust. Scribed: average red rust creep not greater than Rating No. 6 (1.6-3.2 mm / 1/16-1/8 in) per Ref Item No. 15 Annex A, max isolated spot not exceeding 9.5 mm (3/8 in); no separation of coating from substrate强制
6.2.4.9.1Adhesion of alternate corrosion-resistant coating 0.51-1.27 mm (0.020-0.050 in) thick shall exceed coating's own strength via tab-tear testFor coating 0.51-1.27 mm (0.020-0.050 in) thick: two 38-mm (1-1/2-in) circumferential cuts 13 mm (1/2 in) apart to substrate, plus a third perpendicular crossing cut; tab lifted and pulled perpendicular with pliers; tab shall tear rather than coating separating from substrate强制
6.2.4.9.2Adhesion of alternate corrosion-resistant coating 0.127 mm (0.005 in) thick or less determined per referenced standardFor coating 0.127 mm (0.005 in) thick or less: adhesion determined per relevant standard in Ref Item No. 17 Annex A强制
6.2.4.9.3Adhesion of alternate corrosion-resistant coating not covered by 6.2.4.9.1 or 6.2.4.9.2 determined by special investigationDetermined by special investigation强制
A.1 item 8Zinc-coating thinnest-spot (Preece) test reference cited at clause 6.2.2.11US & Canada: ASTM A 239-95 (2004) Standard Practice for Locating the Thinnest Spot in a Zinc (Galvanized) Coating on Iron or Steel Articles; Mexico: NMX-H-013-1984 (Preece Test)强制
C.1Zinc coating required tests (informative overview, see Clause 5.3.1.1)Bend Test (6.2.1.1), Cold Bend (6.2.1.3), Zinc Coating Test (6.2.2)参考
C.1Alternate Corrosion-Resistant coating required tests (informative overview)Bend (6.2.1.1), Cold Bend (6.2.1.3), Ultraviolet Light and Water (6.2.4.5), Salt Spray Fog (6.2.4.6), Moist CO2-SO2-Air (6.2.4.7), Tensile (6.2.4.8), Adhesion (6.2.4.9), Flame Propagation (6.2.4.11)参考
C.1Alternate Corrosion-Resistant Nonmetallic coating additional required tests (in addition to alternate corrosion-resistant list)Electrical Continuity (5.3.3.2); Assembly, Bending, Resistance, Pull, and Fault Current (5.3.3.2); Identification of Compounds (6.2.1.4); Cold Impact (6.2.4.10)参考
C.1Organic coating required tests (informative overview)Bend (6.2.1.1), Cold Bend (6.2.1.3), Identification of Compounds (6.2.1.4), Elasticity (6.2.3.1), Warm Humid Air Test (6.2.3.2)参考
C.1Supplementary Coatings required tests (informative overview)Detrimental Effects to Primary Corrosion-Resistant Coating (5.3.5.2), Fit of Couplings (5.3.5.2), Electrical Continuity (5.3.5.2), Flame Propagation (6.2.4.11)参考
C.1 / 5.3.6.1Surface Treatment coating-test applicability threshold (informative overview)Tests N/A if surface treatment is less than 0.038 mm (0.00015 in) thickness参考
D.1.3Interior of product shall have smooth, continuous zinc or organic coating of uniform appearance over the entire lengthSmooth, continuous zinc or organic coating, uniform appearance throughout entire length; occasional slight roughness (from uneven coating flow) is allowed; complete smooth finish with no rough patches is best evidence of acceptable cleaning, corrosion protection, and absence of scale/burrs/fins/dirt/defects/foreign substances参考
🔬 试验 53 条
条款要求判据 / 限值性质源页
4.1Units of measurement: which values are normative/standard by countryCanada/Mexico: SI (metric) values shall be normative, other values for information only. US: SI or inch-pound regarded separately as standard; each system used independently; do not combine systems强制
5.3.3.2Alternate corrosion-resistant coating compliance and durability testing.Shall comply with Clause 6.2.4. Tubing/elbow shall be subjected to assembly, bending, resistance, pull, and fault current tests (Ref. Item No. 3, Annex A) with both set-screw and compression couplings, without removal of the alternate coating.强制
5.3.5.2Evaluation criteria for supplementary nonmetallic coatings.Shall be evaluated for: a) flame propagation; b) detrimental effects on corrosion resistance of primary coatings; c) fit of couplings; d) electrical continuity with couplings (Ref. Item No. 4, Annex A). Supplementary coating shall be in addition to the full primary corrosion-resistant coating.强制
6.1.2Cold-conditioned (0°C) bend test on smallest available trade size of finished tube.Condition at 0°C (32°F) for 60 min; one specimen of smallest available finished tube shall bend into a quarter circle using bending equipment with centerline radius not less than Table 5.2. Tube shall not develop a crack and weld shall not open. Test conducted inside cold chamber or begun within 15 s of removal. Tubing with nonmetallic alternate corrosion-resistant coating marked below "0°C" conditioned at its rated temperature (any temperature below 0°C in 5°C/9°F increments). Same specimen may also be used for coating test in Clause 6.2.1.3.强制
6.2.2.2Specimen preparation for zinc Preece test: cut 150-mm specimens from finished zinc-coated tube, solvent-clean, select only undamaged specimens; cut handling depends on coating locationSpecimens 150 mm (6 in) long; cleaned with suitable organic solvent; only undamaged specimens used; if zinc on inside and outside, half the specimens cut longitudinally in half to expose inside; if zinc only on outside, none cut longitudinally强制
6.2.2.3Specimens rinsed in water and all surfaces dried with clean cheesecloth before immersion; zinc surface must be dry and clean; specimens not touched by hands or contaminantsRemove as much water as possible (water slows zinc/solution reaction); zinc surface dry and clean before immersion; do not touch with hands or anything that can contaminate/damage surfaces强制
6.2.2.4Immersion conditions for copper sulfate test: beaker diameter ~2× specimen diameter, solution depth, temperature, immersion depth, duration, and no disturbanceBeaker diameter ≈ twice specimen diameter; solution depth ≥ 76 mm (3 in); solution temperature 18.3 ±1.1°C (65.0 ±2.0°F); ≥ 64 mm (2-1/2 in) of specimen length immersed, supported on end at beaker center; immersed 60 s; not moved and solution not stirred during immersion强制
6.2.2.5Post-immersion handling and examination: remove after 60 s, rinse in running tap water, rub off loose copper with cheesecloth, dry; examine immersed part by broad surface, disregarding cut-end and longitudinal-edge zonesDisregard portions within 13 mm (1/2 in) of cut ends for sizes 12–53 (3/8–2) and 25 mm (1 in) for sizes 63–155 (2-1/2–6), and within 3 mm (1/8 in) of any longitudinal cut edges; do not touch immersed surfaces with hands/contaminants强制
6.2.2.6Record bright firmly-adhering copper deposit on the immersed part, excluding the disregarded cut-end and longitudinal cut-edge zonesRecord when immersed part shows any deposit of bright, firmly-adhering copper, exclusive of 13 mm (1/2 in) cut-end portions and any 3 mm (1/8 in) longitudinal cut-edge portions强制
6.2.2.7Repeat immerse/wash/rub/dry/examine/record cycle up to required number of immersions or until copper appears; discard used solution portion after a specimen and use fresh portion for each succeeding specimenRepeat using same specimen and beaker until required immersions reached or copper noted (whichever first); discard solution portion after each specimen; fresh portion for each succeeding specimen强制
6.2.2.10Preparation of copper-coated reference standard for comparison: expose bare steel with hydrochloric acid, then dip in copper sulfate to show character of bright copper deposit on exposed steelPartially submerge zinc-coated specimen in strong hydrochloric acid until violent action ceases; remove/wash/dry; dip exposed bare area (incl. zinc-intact portion) a few seconds in copper sulfate at 16–20°C (61–66°F); wash, dry; prepare reference standard at time of testing强制
6.2.2.11An alternate test method to Clauses 6.2.2.1–6.2.2.10 is permitted per Reference Item No. 8, Annex ASee Reference Item No. 8, Annex A for alternate test method强制
6.2.3.1.2Elasticity test apparatus: flat sheet-steel test pieces, baking oven, and vise with rounded jaw edgesTest pieces 75 by 125 mm (3 by 5 in), ~0.25 mm (0.010 in) thick; oven for baking; vise jaws ≥ 75 mm (3 in) wide; 75-mm jaw edge rounded to radius 1.6 mm (1/16 in)强制
6.2.3.1.3Elasticity test specimen prep and baking: solvent-clean two test pieces, dip in organic coating, air dry, suspend by wires, bake per defined temperatureTwo test pieces solvent-cleaned, dipped in coating, air dried 30 min, suspended by short wires; baked 5 h at normal baking temperature; if normal baking temp < 135°C (275°F) or coating air-dried, maintain oven at 135–150°C (275–302°F)强制
6.2.3.1.4Elasticity bending procedure and pass/fail: cool, then perform 5 cycles of 180° back-and-forth bends over vise jaw edges; coating must not crack, flake, or be damagedCool in still air to room temperature; bend 90° against one jaw edge, back through 180° to opposite jaw edge, then 90° opposite back to original — cycle repeated 5 times; reject if coating on any test piece cracks, flakes off, or is otherwise damaged强制
6.2.3.2.1Warm humid air test apparatus: insulated specimen chamber with water reservoir, pump, spray/humidifying chamber, circulating fan, heating, specimen supports, controlsInsulated chamber inside dimensions ≈ 119 by 71 by 71 cm (47 by 28 by 28 in); contains temperature-controlled water reservoir, pump, spray chamber, air-circulating fan, air heating, specimen supports, controls强制
6.2.3.2.2Warm humid air test conditions: maintain chamber dry-bulb temperature, relative humidity, and specimen support angle throughout testDry bulb 60 ±1°C (140 ±2°F); relative humidity 98 ±2% maintained throughout; specimens supported in racks at 15° from vertical强制
6.2.3.2.3Warm humid air test duration and pass criterion: run for the full period with no corrosion of the metalConduct for 60 d (days); no corrosion of the metal at end of test强制
6.2.4.1Alternate (non-zinc) exterior corrosion-resistant coating shall, in addition to 6.2.4.2–6.2.4.7.3, be evaluated for flame propagation and the tube/elbow subjected to assembly, bending, resistance, pull, and fault-current tests with both coupling typesEvaluate flame propagation (see Clause 6.2.4.11); subject tubing/elbow to assembly, bending, resistance, pull, and fault current tests per Reference Item No. 9, Annex A, with both set-screw and compression-type couplings; plus requirements of Clauses 6.2.4.2–6.2.4.7.3强制
6.2.4.2Alternate coating shall comply with salt-spray (fog), moist CO₂-SO₂-air, and UV-light-and-water tests after conditioning per Clause 6.2.4.4; near-cut-edge corrosion disregardedPass salt-spray (fog), moist carbon dioxide–sulfur dioxide–air, and ultraviolet light and water tests after conditioning per Clause 6.2.4.4; disregard corrosion within 13 mm (1/2 in) of cut edges强制
6.2.4.3.1Thirty specimens with corrosion-resistant coating, of trade size 2 (53) or closest manufactured, shall be tested30 specimens, each 152-203 mm (6-8 in) long, trade size 2 (53) or closest manufactured强制
6.2.4.3.2Half of specimens to be exposed shall be scribed per Figure 3 using a rotary tool until bright base metal exposed; coating thickness measured before exposureRotary tool at 15,000-30,000 rpm; 1.14 mm (0.045 in) thick fiberglass-reinforced cut-off wheel; scribe per Figure 3 until bright base metal exposed; specimens free of grease/dirt; measure coating thickness prior to exposure强制
6.2.4.4.1Six scribed and six unscribed specimens of each coating shall be air-oven conditioned before salt spray and CO2-SO2-air testsCondition 240 h at 100 ±1°C (212 ±2°F) in air-circulating oven; tubing rated above 90°C (200°F) conditioned at rated temperature + 10°C (18°F), rating any temperature above 90°C at 25°C (45°F) increments强制
6.2.4.5.1Three scribed and three unscribed specimens shall be exposed to UV light and water by one of two specified methodsMethod per Clause 6.2.4.5.3 (twin enclosed carbon-arc) or 6.2.4.5.4 (xenon-arc)强制
6.2.4.5.3Twin enclosed carbon-arc UV/water exposure method and apparatus parameters360 h exposure per Ref Item No. 11 Annex A, apparatus type DH; Method 1, continuous light + intermittent water spray; 120-min cycle = 102-min light + 18-min water spray with light; black-panel temperature 63 ±3°C (145 ±5°F)强制
6.2.4.5.4Xenon-arc UV/water exposure method and apparatus parameters500 h exposure per Ref Item No. 12 Annex A, apparatus type BH; Test Method A, continuous light + intermittent water spray; 120-min cycle = 102-min light + 18-min water spray with light; 6500 W water-cooled xenon-arc lamp; borosilicate glass inner and outer optical filters; spectral irradiance 0.35 W/m²/nm at 340 nm; black-panel temperature 63 ±3°C (145 ±5°F)强制
6.2.4.6.1Six as-received and six air-oven conditioned specimens shall be exposed to salt spray (fog)600 h salt spray (fog) per Ref Item No. 13 Annex A; three as-received and three air-oven conditioned specimens scribed per Clause 6.2.4.3.2强制
6.2.4.7.1Six as-received and six air-oven conditioned specimens shall be exposed to moist CO2-SO2-air; chamber specification1200 h exposure; chamber volume at least 0.085 m³ (3 ft³) with water jacket and thermostatically controlled heater maintaining 35 ±2°C (95 ±3°F); three as-received and three air-oven conditioned specimens scribed per Clause 6.2.4.3.2强制
6.2.4.10.1Cold impact test - conditioning and pass criterion for nonmetallic alternate corrosion-resistant coatingTen specimens 152-203 mm (6-8 in) of finished tubing conditioned at 0°C (32°F) for 60 min (tubing rated below 0°C conditioned at rated temperature, rating any temperature below 0°C in 5°C/9°F increments); then impact of 12.2 J (9 ft-lb) per Clause 6.2.4.10.2; coating shall not separate from metal nor be damaged so bare metal is exposed强制
6.2.4.10.2Cold impact test execution method and timingUse Tup B falling mass with apparatus and method of Ref Item No. 18 Annex A; test conducted inside cold chamber or within 15 s of removal from cold chamber强制
6.2.4.10.3Alternative height/weight combination permitted for cold impactAny height and weight combination yielding the same impact force as Clause 6.2.4.10.1 may be used when the impact face remains unchanged强制
6.2.4.11.1Flammability - vertical specimens with nonmetallic alternate coating shall not flame beyond limit after flame applications; particle, flame-duration, and coating-consumption criteriaShall not flame longer than 5 s following any of three 60-s flame applications, with 30 s between applications. Shall not: a) emit flaming/glowing particles or flaming drops that ignite cotton on burner/wedge/floor (flameless charring ignored); b) continue to flame longer than 5 s after any flame application; c) have coating consumed during or after any flame application强制
6.2.4.11.2Flammability test setup - number of specimens, enclosure dimensions, specimen mounting, cotton layer, and geometry3 unaged specimens tested separately, each in a 3-sided metal enclosure in exhaust hood/cabinet; enclosure 305 mm (12 in) wide x 355 mm (14 in) deep x 610 mm (24 in) high, top and front open; 457-mm (18-in) finished tubing specimen secured longitudinal axis vertical in center; flat horizontal untreated surgical cotton layer 6-25 mm (1/4-1 in) thick on floor; upper cotton surface 229-241 mm (9-9-1/2 in) below point B (tip of blue inner cone of test flame touching specimen, Figure 4)强制
6.2.4.11.3Flammability test burner specification and flame calibrationBurner per Ref Item No. 19 Annex A, bore 9.5 ±0.3 mm, length 100 ±10 mm from top of air inlet openings to top of mixing tube, or equivalent meeting calibration of Ref Item No. 20 Annex A; with barrel vertical and burner well away from specimen, overall flame height adjusted to approximately 100-125 mm (4-5 in); blue inner cone 38 mm (1-1/2 in) high; temperature at tip 816°C (1500°F) or higher measured with chromel-alumel (nickel-chromium and nickel-manganese-aluminum) thermocouple; without disturbing flame-height adjustments, close burner-flame gas valve and separate pilot-flame gas valve强制
6.2.4.11.4Gas supply for the burner (flame test) must be bottled, technical-grade methane or natural gas, with a regulator and meter for uniform gas flowApproximately 37 MJ/m³ (1000 Btu/ft³) at normal pressure; suitable regulator and meter for uniform gas flow强制
6.2.4.11.5Burner positioning for flame test: wedge tilts barrel 20° from vertical (per Figure 5), burner secured to wedge in adjustable support jig, surgical cotton layer on wedge and around burner base; barrel longitudinal axis in the vertical plane containing the specimen axis, parallel to enclosure sides; point A positioned 38 mm from point BTilt 20° from vertical; untreated surgical cotton 6–25 mm (1/4–1 in) thick; point A (intersection of barrel axis with plane of barrel tip) located 38 mm (1-1/2 in) from point B (where extended barrel axis meets outer surface of specimen, where tip of blue inner cone touches center of front of specimen); point B kept no closer than 76 mm (3 in) to the lower clamp/support强制
6.2.4.11.6Where the burner has no gas pilot light, the burner/wedge support shall allow quick removal and precise return to the test position without disturbing the cotton on the enclosure floor or on the wedge/burner baseBurner can be quickly removed from and precisely returned to the position of Clause 6.2.4.11.5 without disturbing the cotton layers强制
6.2.4.11.7Burner ignition procedure (pilot vs. no-pilot) and test environment: where a pilot light exists, open pilot gas valve and light it; where no pilot, support burner away from specimen and light it; conduct the operation and remainder of test under a forced-draft exhaust hood/cabinetForced-draft exhaust hood or cabinet removes smoke and fumes without drafts that affect the flame强制
6.2.4.11.8Flame application cycle for burner with gas pilot: open burner gas valve to apply flame automatically, hold open then close, and repeat for total of 3 applicationsValve held open for 60 s, then closed for 30 s; procedure repeated twice for a total of 3 applications of flame to the specimen强制
Figure 3Scribe pattern for coating/adhesion test specimen (referenced by Clause 6.2.4.3.2): vertical scribe lines in groups a-b-a with specified scribed-area height, line spacing, and scribe-width rangesOverall scribed-area height 64 mm (2-1/2 in); horizontal spacing between adjacent scribe lines 6.4 mm (1/4 in); a = 1.3 mm (0.05 in) – 2.5 mm (0.1 in); b = 3.2 mm (0.125 in) – 5 mm (0.2 in)强制
6.2.4.11.2Figure 4 defines the essential apparatus geometry for the vertical flame test (drawing SC1808B): specimen mounted vertically with burner barrel positioned relative to specimenBurner barrel oriented at 20° to specimen; reference planes: plane of tip of barrel and vertical plane parallel to enclosure sides containing axis of specimen and axis of barrel强制
A.1 item 10Corrosive-environment coated-specimen evaluation reference cited at clause 6.2.4.5.2US & Canada: ASTM D 1654-05, Procedure A, Method 2, Evaluation of Painted or Coated Specimens Subjected to Corrosive Environments; Mexico: NMX-J-023/1-ANCE-2000强制
A.1 item 11Enclosed carbon-arc light exposure (weathering) reference cited at clause 6.2.4.5.3US & Canada: ASTM G 153-04 Operating Enclosed Carbon Arc Light Apparatus for Exposure of Nonmetallic Materials; Mexico: NMX-J-023/1-ANCE-2000强制
A.1 item 12Xenon-arc light exposure (weathering) reference cited at clause 6.2.4.5.4US & Canada: ASTM G 155-05, Cycle 1, Operating Xenon Arc Light Apparatus for Exposure of Non-Metallic Materials; Mexico: NMX-J-023/1-ANCE-2000强制
6.2.4.6.1Salt spray (fog) corrosion test apparatus/operation per referenced standard (alternate corrosion-resistant coating)Per ASTM B 117-03 (US/Canada) / NMX-D-022-1973 (Mexico), Standard Practice for Operating Salt Spray (Fog) Apparatus参考
6.2.4.6.2Evaluation of salt-spray-exposed coated specimens per referenced methodPer ASTM D 1654-05, Procedure A, Method 2 (US/Canada) / NMX-J-023/1-ANCE-2000 (Mexico)参考
6.2.4.7.2Evaluation of moist CO2-SO2-air-exposed coated specimens per referenced methodPer ASTM D 1654-05, Procedure A, Method 2 (US/Canada) / NMX-J-023/1-ANCE-2000 (Mexico)参考
6.2.4.8.1Tensile properties test method for plastics (alternate nonmetallic coating)Per ASTM D 638-03 (US/Canada) / NMX-E-082-2002 (Mexico), Tensile Properties of Plastics参考
6.2.4.9.2Adhesion measurement by tape test (alternate coating)Per ASTM D 3359-02 (US/Canada) / NMX-H-013-1984 Preece Test (Mexico), Measuring Adhesion by Tape Test参考
6.2.4.10.2Cold impact resistance test method (tup/falling weight) for thermoplastic pipe/fittingsPer ASTM D 2444-99 (R2005) (US/Canada) / NMX-E-012-SCFI-1999 (Mexico), Impact Resistance by Means of a Tup (Falling Weight)参考
6.2.4.11.3Laboratory burner specification for small-scale burning/flame-propagation testsPer ASTM D 5025-05 (US/Canada) / NMX-J-192-ANCE-1999 (Mexico), Laboratory Burner for Small-Scale Burning Tests参考
6.2.4.11.3Confirmation of test flames (20-mm/50-W and 125-mm/500-W) for small-scale burning testsPer ASTM D 5207-03 (US/Canada) / NMX-J-192-ANCE-1999 (Mexico); confirm 20-mm (50-W) and 125-mm (500-W) test flames参考
D.1.2Method for examining interior surfaces of EMTLook through the product first from one end and then from the other, at an artificial light参考
⚙ 工艺 9 条
条款要求判据 / 限值性质源页
5.1.1Tube material, form, and seam weldingEach tube shall be of steel, straight, circular cross-section; all seams shall be thoroughly welded强制
5.1.3Surface condition before protective coating is appliedInterior and exterior surfaces shall be free from scale, flash, or any other protrusion, other than the bead allowed per 5.1.2强制
5.1.5Threading restriction on EMTEMT shall not be threaded unless integral fittings (per 5.5) are steel and the thread is not of standard pipe-thread dimensions强制
5.4.1Elbows made from same grade of tubing as straight EMT and produced to EMT requirements.Shall be made from the same grade of tubing as straight EMT and treated, coated, and otherwise produced per EMT requirements. See Annex D.强制
5.4.2Optional integral coupling/fitting on elbow ends must comply.Elbow may have integral coupling/fitting on one or both ends; each fitting shall comply with Reference Item No. 5, Annex A.强制
5.4.3Elbow bend formation, smoothness, minimum radius, and straight-end length.Curve formed with suitable bending equipment; centerline radius not less than Table 5.2. Curved portion shall be smooth and continuous throughout the bend without creases (visual exam under artificial light, normal/corrected vision). Elbows sharper than 90° excluded. Straight-end length L₅ not smaller than Table 5.2. See Figure 1.强制
5.5.1Expanded-end / integral fitting on EMT must comply with relevant fittings standard.Each integral fitting (e.g. expanded end) shall comply with the relevant standard for fittings for conduit, tubing, and cable per Reference Item No. 6, Annex A.强制
D.1.1Manufacturer shall visually examine each length of EMT on both exterior and interior surfaces for defectsDetermine absence or presence of poor coatings, hard scale, burrs or fins, or other defects; visual examination of both exterior and interior surfaces of each length参考
D.1.4Elbows shall be examined as straight lengths before bending and monitored during bendingExamine elbows per Clause 5.4.1 as straight lengths before bending; additionally monitor elbows while being bent for evidence of flaking and other damage参考
🏷 标记 17 条
条款要求判据 / 限值性质源页
5.3.3.3Upper ambient temperature use/marking for nonmetallic alternate corrosion-resistant coating.If not marked with a temperature designation, or marked "90°C" ("200°F"), use is for ambient ≤90°C (200°F). Higher-temperature ratings may be marked if evaluated per Clause 6.2.4.4.1.强制
5.3.3.4Lower ambient temperature use/marking for nonmetallic alternate corrosion-resistant coating.If not marked with a temperature designation, or marked "0°C" ("32°F"), use is for ambient ≥0°C (32°F). Below-0°C ratings may be marked if evaluated per Clauses 6.2.1.3 and 6.2.4.10.强制
5.3.5.1Supplementary coatings permitted but unevaluated ones must be marked.One or more supplementary coatings not prohibited; not required to meet primary corrosion-resistant coating requirements. Tubing/elbows with supplementary coatings not evaluated for corrosion resistance shall be marked per Clause 7.7.强制
7 (Advisory Note)Advisory note on bilingual markings in Canada (English/French); Annex E provides translations; markings may be in other languages to conform with local language requirementsAll markings required by this Standard may be in other languages to conform with the language requirements where the product is to be used (informative advisory)参考
7.1Each finished straight length of EMT and each factory elbow shall be marked to identify the responsible organizationMarked with manufacturer's name, trade name, both, or other distinctive marking by which the responsible organization can be readily identified强制
7.2In the US, when tubing/elbows are produced in more than one factory, each finished length and each elbow must carry a distinctive (may be coded) factory-identifying marking; does not apply in Canada and MexicoUS: distinctive marking identifying product of a particular factory (may be in code). Canada and Mexico: requirement does not apply强制
7.3Each straight length of tubing and each elbow shall be legibly and durably marked with the product designationMarked "Electrical Metallic Tubing" or "EMT" by durable means (die stamping, ink stamping with etching ink, or paint); letters at least 3 mm (1/8 in) high强制
7.4Each finished length of tubing or elbow shall carry a legible, durable installation-advisory markingMarked "Consult manufacturer for proper installation" or equivalent wording强制
7.5Maximum-use-temperature marking for tubing with nonmetallic alternate corrosion-resistant coating; higher-temperature-rated tubing marked with evaluated rated temperature, at a specified marking frequencyMay be marked max use temp "90°C" ("200°F"); if tested above 90°C (200°F), marked "____°C" ("____°F") with rated temp per Clause 6.2.4.4.1; marked at minimum once every 3.05 m (10 ft) and no less than once every piece强制
7.6Minimum-use-temperature marking for tubing with nonmetallic alternate corrosion-resistant coating; lower-temperature-rated tubing marked with evaluated rated temperature, at a specified marking frequencyMay be marked min use temp "0°C" ("32°F"); if tested below 0°C (32°F), marked "____°C" ("____°F") with rated temp per Clause 6.2.1.3; marked at minimum once every 3.05 m (10 ft) and no less than once every piece强制
7.7Tubing/elbows with supplementary coating not evaluated for corrosion protection shall carry a disclaimer marking naming the coating typeMarked "Corrosion protection properties of the ______________ coating were not investigated" or equivalent, with the blank filled in with the type of supplementary coating强制
7.8Each master bundle tag (tubing) or carton/package (elbows) shall carry a date/dating-period of manufacture marking; defines manufacturing date for elbows/bendsDistinctive marking indicating date or dating period of manufacture not exceeding any three consecutive months. For elbow/bend, date is: (a) date tube made and bent at same location, or (b) date elbow/bend formed when tubing made at different location强制
7.9Date of manufacture may be abbreviated in a code subject to constraintsCode must not (a) repeat in less than 20 years, and (b) require reference to manufacturer's production records to determine manufacturing date强制
E.1.1Products for specific national applications may bear alternative markings to those in Clause 7Permissive — alternative national markings (per Table E1) may be used in place of Clause 7 markings参考
7.3 (Table E1)Alternative national marking for product designation in English/Spanish/FrenchEnglish: 'Electrical Metallic Tubing'; Spanish: 'Tubo metálico rígido de acero tipo ligero'; French: 'Tube électrique métallique'参考
7.4 (Table E1)Alternative national marking for installation instruction referenceEnglish: 'Consult manufacturer for proper installation'; Spanish: 'Consultar con el fabricante sobre los requisitos de instalación'; French: 'Consulter les directives d'installation du fabricant'参考
7.7 (Table E1)Alternative national marking for non-investigated corrosion protection of coatingEnglish: 'Corrosion protection properties of the ____ coating were not investigated' (with Spanish/French equivalents); blank to be filled with coating type参考
📎 其他 18 条
条款要求判据 / 限值性质源页
Application of Standard (Preface)Where a specific number of test samples is referenced, that number is a minimumSpecified number is to be considered a minimum quantity参考
1.1Scope: products covered (EMT and elbows as metal raceway)Covers steel EMT and elbows for use as metal raceway for installation of wires/cables per CSA C22.1, NOM-001-SEDE, NFPA 70; user responsible for determining appropriate product强制
1.2Scope exclusion: aluminum EMTAluminum EMT is covered by standards in Reference Item No. 2, Annex A (not this standard)强制
2.1Compliance with reference installation codes per country of useProducts shall comply with reference installation codes/standards for the country of use; if multi-country, shall comply with all relevant countries' codes (see Annex A)强制
2.2Interpretation of dated vs undated normative referencesUndated references refer to latest edition plus all revisions up to approval; dated references refer to that dated edition plus all revisions up to approval强制
3.5Definition: elbowA manufactured curved section of EMT强制
3.6Definition: electrical metallic tubing - steel (EMT)Unthreaded steel raceway of circular cross-section for physical protection/routing of wire conductors and for use as equipment grounding conductor when installed with appropriate fittings强制
6.1.3Clause 6.1.3 is deleted.Deleted (no requirement).参考
6.2.1.5Supplementary coating described in Clause 5.3.5 is exempt from the bend (crack/flake) requirement of 6.2.1.1Supplementary coating (Clause 5.3.5) need not comply with 6.2.1.1强制
6.2.4.11.1 (Note)Note: thin nonmetallic topcoat (lacquer/paint) ≤0.125 mm over confirmed 3 dips of zinc may show total topcoat consumption at flame-contact point without flame propagation if 6.2.4.11.1 a) and b) are metTopcoat ≤0.125 mm (0.005 in) thick over confirmed 3 dips of zinc; total topcoat consumption allowed at flame-contact point with no measurable flame propagation, provided 6.2.4.11.1 a) and b) met; presence of 3 dips of zinc confirmed using Zinc Coating test 6.2.2 as a separate test参考
Figure 2Figure 2 (Test apparatus for bending EMT) is deletedFigure deleted参考
A.1Annex A (normative) §A.1 lists the normative references invoked by the standard, mapped per country (US / Canada / Mexico) with the clause where each is referenced (See Clause 2.1). Reference items 1–12 shown; table continues onto next pageNormative reference table; each row binds a referenced document (e.g. national electrical codes, UL 514B / CSA C22.2 No.18.3 fittings, UL 746A / CSA C22.2 No.0.17 polymeric evaluation) to the citing clause强制
A.1 item 1Installation code reference cited at clause 1.1US: NFPA 70-2017 National Electrical Code; Canada: CSA C22.1 Canadian Electrical Code; Mexico: NOM-001-SEDE Standard for Electrical Installations强制
A.1 item 2Aluminum EMT scope reference cited at clause 1.2US: UL 797A Electrical Metallic Tubing – Aluminum; Canada: CSA C22.2 No.83 Electrical Metallic Tubing; Mexico: NMX-576-ANCE-2005 Aluminum Rigid Conduit强制
A.1 item 3Fittings reference cited at clause 5.3.3.2US: UL 514B Conduit, Tubing and Cable Fittings; Canada: C22.2 No.18.3; Mexico: NMX-J-017-ANCE-2005强制
A.1 item 5Fittings reference cited at clause 5.4.2US: UL 514B; Canada: CSA C22.2 No.18.3; Mexico: NMX-J-017-ANCE-2005强制
A.1 item 6Fittings reference cited at clause 5.5.1US: UL 514B; Canada: C22.2 No.18.3; Mexico: NMX-J-017-ANCE-2005强制
A.1 item 9Fittings reference cited at clause 6.2.4.1US: UL 514B; Canada: CSA C22.2 No.18.3; Mexico: NMX-J-017-ANCE-2005强制

图内容源页
Figure 1弯头几何（弯角 90°±2°、弯曲半径 R、开口端径 d；§5.4.3 引用）
Figure 3试样划线图样（§6.2.4.3.2；划线区高 64mm/2-1/2in）
Figure 4阻燃试验装置几何（喷灯 20° 倾斜、各基准面；§6.2.4.11.2）
Figure 5楔块尺寸 mm(in)（图样 SB1214C；§6.2.4.11.5）
Figure 2【已删除】弯管试验装置（标准中标注 Deleted）
✅ 数值对抗校验：24 个 agent 转录 + 独立回读原页逐数值核对。所有尺寸/公差/温度/时长/判据数值均与源页一致。人工复核并修正 3 处页码引用：6.2.3.1.1–.1.4 → p14（§6.2.3 自 p14 起）、7.5–7.9 → p19（7.1–7.4 在 p18）、Figure 3 → p21。Table 5.1/5.2/B.1/F1 全部数值双重确认无误（仅上方 5″/6″ 公制重量为标准本身疑误）。
相关：测试要求详解 →

📊 标准全景与边界

📊 标准全景与边界（背景分析 · 核心结论 / 一图速览 / CSA 差异 / 完整性自检）
核心结论：cULus 认证基于 UL 797（美国，第9版 2023-04-28）+ CSA C22.2 No.83.1（加拿大）两标准。2023-04-28 两标准与墨西哥 NMX-J-536-ANCE 完成联合修订，成为 tri-national 三国统一标准，技术要求完全一致。CSA 版与 UL 797 没有任何额外或更严的机械/化学要求，差异仅在标志引用规范（CSA 引 CEC/C22.1，UL 引 NEC/NFPA 70，墨西哥引 NOM-001-SEDE）。尺寸规范 ANSI C80.3 作为规范性引用文件被 UL 797 引用。一次 cULus 申请同时覆盖三国市场。
3
统一标准国家（US/CA/MX）
第9版
UL 797 现行版本（2023）
1/2″–4″
钢制 EMT 主力贸易规格
0
抗拉/抗压/抗冲击门槛
关键提示（极重要）：UL 797 对基材【没有】规定明确的最低抗拉/屈服强度数值，也【没有】抗压（crush）、抗冲击（impact）、跌落等机械破坏性试验——这与刚性金属导管（UL 6 RMC）等明显不同。EMT 的机械合格性主要由 弯曲试验 + 尺寸公差 + 焊缝完整性 三者保证。

UL 797 对 EMT“有要求 / 无要求”一图速览

图：EMT 的合格性靠成型/尺寸/焊缝/镀层化学测试保证，而非强度数值或破坏性力学试验。

关于壁厚：标准对壁厚是 +10% / −5% 公差（并非“无要求”），但没有单独的最小壁厚机械强度门槛——这正回应“标准对壁厚没特别要求、焊管 ±5%?”的疑问：壁厚有公差约束，但不通过强度数值来卡，而是用公差带 + 弯曲试验 + 镀层试验综合保证。
硫酸铜（Preece）最易失败项 — 必读：这是 EMT 认证中最容易失败的测试，焊缝区最危险（焊接热影响区镀锌往往偏薄）。判读关键：深色 / 黑色铜斑不算失败，只有光亮、牢固附着的金属铜才算失败——意味着该处锌层已被消耗殆尽、露出钢基。溶液比重 1.183–1.189 @ 18.3°C（65.0°F）。

🇨🇦 CSA C22.2 No.83.1 差异说明

与 UL 797 同为一份三国统一文件，测试章节与判据完全相同。唯一差异是标志引用的规范（加拿大引 CSA C22.1 / CEC，美国引 NFPA 70 / NEC，墨西哥引 NOM-001-SEDE）与认证标志（cUL vs UL）。没有 CSA 专属的额外化学成分或机械限制。
📑 三国标志引用规范速查
国家统一标准编号安装规范引用认证标志
🇺🇸 美国UL 797NEC / NFPA 70（Art. 358）UL Listed
🇨🇦 加拿大CSA C22.2 No.83.1CEC / CSA C22.1cUL
🇲🇽 墨西哥NMX-J-536-ANCENOM-001-SEDENOM

三标准技术要求完全一致，一次 cULus 申请同时覆盖。

完整性自检与边界

✅ 已确认「标准本就不要求」的项（印证我们的判断）

常被误以为有的要求实际
抗压 / 抗冲击试验（crush / impact）UL 797 无此试验（与刚性导管 UL 6 不同）
最低抗拉 / 屈服强度门槛无强度数值门槛，由弯曲 + 尺寸 + 焊缝把关
标志耐久性 / 摩擦测试无独立标志永久性试验

📎 边界澄清（属于其它标准，非 UL 797）

事项归属
接头 / 连接器（set-screw / 压接 coupling、recess、螺纹尺寸）UL 514B《Conduit, Tubing, and Cable Fittings》单独评估；UL 797 仅含直管 + 弯头（整体接头例外见 §5.1.5 / §5.4）
作为设备接地导体（EGC）的接地连续性NEC 250.118 认定，非 UL 797 裸管电阻/连续性试验——"无连续性试验"是正确的，不是缺漏
安装（固定间距、用途许可）NEC Article 358

参考来源 References 17 · 官方域

以下为 EMT 相关属性 / 试验 / 基材的权威来源，均为官方标准机构或行业协会域名（标准正文多为付费，链接为官方页/范围/技术资料）。

标准本体

  • UL 797 第9版（Electrical Metallic Tubing-Steel）— UL Standards 官方 — 标准本体 scope（外锌/替代涂层、内有机/锌涂层）。
  • UL 797 Ed.9 — ANSI Webstore — 佐证 ANSI 批准日 2023-04-28。
  • ANSI C80.3-2020 目录与范围（NEMA 官方 PDF） — EMT-S 尺寸/重量/壁厚/90°弯头机械规格。
  • ANSI C80.3-2020 — ANSI Webstore — 尺寸与机械要求，10ft 标准长度。
  • CSA C22.2 No.83.1（三国统一）— CSA Group 官方 — 加拿大 EMT，与 UL 797 / NMX-J-536-ANCE 谐调。
  • CSA C22.2 No.83.1-07 (R2022) — ANSI Webstore — 佐证三国统一标准身份。

试验方法

  • ASTM A239 硫酸铜（Preece）试验 — AGA 知识库 — 浸渍法定位锌层最薄点的方法/局限。
  • ASTM A90/A90M-21 镀层重量测定 — ASTM 官方 — 化学剥离称重法测锌层单位面积重量。

基材钢规范

  • ASTM A1011/A1011M-23（含 SS Gr.33）— ASTM 官方 — EMT 常用热轧碳钢基材，化学 + 力学。
  • JIS G3141（SPCC，冷轧碳钢板带）— JISC 官方门户 — SPCC 商用级基材标准化机构入口。
  • GB/T 700-2006《碳素结构钢》（Q195）— 国标全文公开系统 — Q195/Q215/Q235 化学 + 力学（Q195 屈服≥195MPa）。

热镀锌相容性

  • 推荐钢化学成分对热镀锌镀层的影响 — AGA — Si/P 等使钢成"反应性钢"、含 Cr/Si 相容性问题。
  • 热镀锌镀层结构与性能 — AGA — 锌铁合金分层结构、成分对镀层冶金的影响。

安装 / 接地 / 行业资料

  • NFPA 70 NEC（Article 358 / 接地连续性）— NFPA 官方 — EMT 安装与有效接地连续性背景。
  • 钢导管 / EMT 满足 NEC 接地 — NEMA 技术文档 — 等电位接地与连续性达标论证。
  • EMT 总览 — Steel Tube Institute — 定义、各 trade size 尺寸/重量、内外涂层、归口 NEC 358。
  • EMT 腐蚀防护技术专栏 — Steel Tube Institute — 镀锌/有机涂层防腐、硫酸铜判定、埋设补充防腐。

🎯 我方策略与认证路线图

自制焊管 → EMT 倒推要求 · 现状 · 关键差距 · 我的建议

本页要点：我方路线 = 自制 ERW 焊管 + 自有涂层。下面从 EMT（UL 797 / ANSI C80.3）要求倒推，逐项列出焊管本体与涂层工艺必须保证的机械/化学属性与控制点；其后为现状、关键差距与我的建议。

① 自制焊管 → EMT 倒推要求 逐项控制表

1️⃣ 焊管本体 · 机械属性（倒推自 §6.1 弯曲 + §5.1 焊缝）

EMT 要求（倒推源）焊管须保证控制点 / 验证风险
弯曲后焊缝不开裂、不张开（§6.1）焊缝延展性接近母材高频 ERW（热影响区窄）；焊后正火 / 退火消残余应力焊后未退火 → 低温弯曲焊缝脆裂
常温 + 低温 0°C 弯 ¼ 圆弧 → 管/焊缝不裂、截面不变形（§6.1）钢材伸长率 ≥20%、屈强比低选 Q195 / Q215 / SPCC；查伸长率与回弹高屈强比 → 回弹大、开裂
内壁焊瘤 ≤0.38mm(½–2″)/≤0.51mm(2½–4″)（§5.1.2）焊缝内毛刺刮平在线刮刀去内焊瘤 + 抽检瘤高瘤过高刮伤导线
截面不变形 / 成品重量达标（Table 5.1）壁厚均匀核验壁厚公差，避免局部过薄局部薄 → 截面畸变 / 重量不足

2️⃣ 焊管本体 · 化学属性（按基材牌号 A1011/SPCC + 镀锌相容；UL 797 不规定成分）

EMT 要求焊管须保证控制点
碳素结构钢、可冷弯低碳 C ≤0.15%、低 Mn用 SPCC / Q195 / A1011 SS33 牌号
镀锌后过硫酸铜（不露铁）无 Cr/Mo、低 Si核验材质单 Cr/Mo/Si 残余（Cr 形成钝化膜阻碍锌润湿）
焊接无需预热碳当量 CE 低（<0.25）低碳即保证，避免合金钢
P/S 控制P/S 尽量 ≤0.035 / 0.040SPCC 上限略松（0.050），需选批把关

3️⃣ 涂层前 · 表面处理（倒推自 §5.1.3 — 最易踩坑）

环节必须保证风险
除鳞 / 除锈喷丸或酸洗彻底去轧制氧化皮残留氧化皮 → 涂层附着差 → 硫酸铜/盐雾失败（最高风险）
酸洗后处理充分水洗 + 钝化、除残酸残酸 → 底部腐蚀（自制路线最易忽略）
内表面处理内壁清洁、无油污 / 铁锈有机涂层起泡（暖湿空气 60°C/98%RH 试验暴露）
端口去毛刺切管后端口去毛刺、方正毛刺刮伤涂层设备 / 导线

4️⃣ 涂层工艺控制（按路线）

🛡️ 路线 A：外锌 + 内有机（推荐 · 最简）
外锌层电镀 / 热浸 / 吹镀，过硫酸铜不露铁；内角 / 焊缝区防偏薄
内有机涂层膜厚均匀（细管死角难）；烘干温度 / 时间足，防欠固化起泡
锌层附着力表面处理彻底 = 附着力根本
⚠️ 路线 B：外部替代涂层（非金属 · 更严）
腐蚀三试验盐雾 600h + CO₂-SO₂ + 氙弧 / UV 全过
阻燃火焰传播试验
配件组装不去涂层状态过拉拔 / 弯曲 / 接地连续性；涂层厚度须匹配配件间隙

5️⃣ 尺寸管控（倒推自 ANSI C80.3 + §5.1）

关键尺寸风险 / 注意
外径公差 ±0.13mm（½–2″）超差 → 配件无法装配（常见合规问题）
成品重量（最低值）采购焊管时预留涂层重量，避免管壁偏薄 → 重量不足
管端方正度斜口 → 现场配件密封性差

6️⃣ 风险排序（踩坑概率 高 → 低）

#失效链对应试验
1表面处理不彻底 → 涂层附着不足硫酸铜 / 盐雾试验失败
2焊缝脆性（焊后未退火）低温弯曲开裂
3内有机涂层不均暖湿空气起泡腐蚀
4外径公差超差配件装配试验不合格
5涂层厚度与配件间隙不匹配替代涂层路线配件组装失败
一句话倒推结论：自制焊管能否做成合格 EMT，七成看表面处理 + 焊缝退火（决定涂层附着与低温弯曲），三成看基材选对（低碳无 Cr）与尺寸控住（配件可装）。基材见 🔩 钢材选型，试验细节见 🧪 测试要求。

② 焊管须满足的全部属性 采购 / 验收规格表

下表是给焊管厂的采购技术要求——把 EMT 标准翻译成焊管本体可验收的目标值，逐项给「目标值 + 依据/原因」。

A. 化学成分（熔炼分析）

元素 / 指标目标值依据 / 原因
C 碳≤0.15%低碳 = 可冷弯 / 手弯；满足碳素结构钢定义
Mn 锰≤0.60%低锰，常规低碳钢范围
Si 硅≤0.15%避开 Sandelin 区，防热镀锌涂层异常增厚 / 附着差
P 磷≤0.035%杂质控制（A1011 / Q195 基准）
S 硫≤0.040%杂质控制
Cr 铬无★ Cr 形成 Cr₂O₃ 钝化膜阻碍锌润湿——必须避免
Mo 钼无合金元素，碳素钢中不应出现
碳当量 CE≤0.25ERW 高速焊免预热
推荐牌号SPCC / Q195 / A1011 SS Gr.33同族低碳软钢；SPCC 为内枪管同料

B. 机械性能

项目目标值 / 判据依据
抗拉强度~270–360 MPa（无硬门槛）UL 797 不设强度门槛；软利手弯（要顶 360 用半硬态）
屈服强度~170–250 MPa，屈强比低低屈强比 = 回弹小、易弯
伸长率≥20%（建议 ≥25%）常温 + 低温弯曲不裂的隐性要求
常温弯曲弯 90°(¼圆弧) → 管 / 焊缝不裂、截面不变形§6.1.1
低温弯曲0°C/60min，15s 内弯 90° → 不裂§6.1.2（½ / ¾ / 1″）
硬度 / 状态软退火态现场手弯安装

C. 焊缝与表面

项目目标依据 / 风险
焊接方式高频 ERW，焊缝完全焊透§5.1.1；热影响区窄、延展好
焊后处理正火 / 退火消残余应力★ 否则低温弯曲焊缝脆裂
内壁焊瘤≤0.38mm(½–2″) / ≤0.51mm(2½–4″)§5.1.2；过高刮伤导线
焊缝 / 表面外观无金属毛刺、锐边、锐利突起§5.1.2
涂覆前表面无氧化皮、飞边、突出物§5.1.3；残留 → 涂层附着失败
管端切割方正、去毛刺 / 倒角§5.1.4 / §6.3

D. 尺寸（ANSI C80.3 · 常用规格）

规格外径 OD(mm)壁厚(mm)OD 公差
½″17.931.07±0.13
¾″23.421.24±0.13
1″29.541.45±0.13
1½″44.201.65±0.13
2″55.801.65±0.13
壁厚公差 +10% / −5%；长度 10ft(3050mm) ±¼″(±6.35mm)；单位长度重量须达 ANSI C80.3 Table 1 标称值（采购焊管时预留涂层重量）。完整 ½″–4″ 十规格表见 📋 适用标准。
给焊管厂一句话规格：低碳钢（SPCC / Q195，C≤0.15、无 Cr/Mo、Si≤0.15）、高频 ERW 焊后退火、伸长率 ≥20%、壁厚 ±0.1mm、OD 按 ANSI C80.3、内焊瘤 ≤0.38mm、涂覆前彻底除鳞。

③ 工艺流程与阶段控制点 含弯头 · 重点

从带钢到成品 EMT 的完整工序，标出每阶段特别要盯的点。★ = 高风险阶段（漏控直接导致认证失败）；弯头在镀锌之后单独弯制。

①带钢/分条→ ②ERW成管→ ③去焊缝→ ★④焊后退火→ ⑤定径矫直→ ⑥切断·端处理→ ★⑦表面前处理→ ★⑧镀锌250g→ ⑨内涂·烘干→ ⑩打标→ ⑪弯头弯制→ ⑫成品检验
#阶段特别注意点（达成目标）风险 / 关联试验
1带钢准备 / 分条选低碳钢（SPCC/Q195）、无 Cr/Mo、Si≤0.15；分条到管坯宽度化学不符 → 镀锌失败
2ERW 成管高频电阻焊、焊缝完全焊透；参数稳定、热影响区窄虚焊/未焊透 → 弯曲焊缝开裂
3去焊缝（内外刮瘤）内焊瘤 ≤0.38mm(½–2″)/≤0.51mm(2½–4″)、外缘平滑瘤高刮导线；去后焊缝见光→必须焊后镀
★4焊后退火 / 正火消除焊接残余应力、恢复焊缝延展性漏做 → 低温弯曲焊缝脆裂（最易忽略）
5定径 / 矫直OD ±0.13mm超差 → 配件装不上
6切断 + 端处理切 10ft、端口方正、去毛刺 / 倒角（§6.3）斜口/毛刺 → 密封差、刮涂层
★7表面前处理喷丸/酸洗彻底除氧化皮 → 水洗 → 钝化；内壁清洁不彻底 → 涂层附着失败 → 硫酸铜/盐雾不过（最高风险）
★8镀锌（250g 吹镀）外锌主防腐；焊缝区/内角防偏薄、锌层连续达标焊缝处偏薄 → 硫酸铜露铁失败
9内壁有机涂层 + 烘干膜厚均匀（细管死角）、烘干充分欠固化 → 暖湿空气试验起泡
10打标（Marking）字高 ≥3mm、含「EMT」+ cULus、间距 ≤3.05m漏标/字小 → 标志不合规
★11弯头弯制（同级成品管）≤90°、±2°、R/Ls 按 Table 5.2；弯曲外侧镀层/涂层不裂、焊缝不开镀层弯裂/焊缝开 → 涂层柔韧/弯曲试验失败
12成品检验 / 出厂尺寸 + 硫酸铜内部自测（送检前置） + 弯曲抽检漏检 → 批量返工
🔧 弯头（Elbow）在什么阶段？要特别注意什么？
  • 身份：弯头是 UL 797 范围内的产品（与直管一同认证，§5.4），不是外购配件——用同级钢材、同处理、同涂层。
  • 在哪个阶段：主线之后单独弯制，两种工艺——① 成品镀锌管再弯（主流）：弯曲外侧镀层/涂层须过 §6.2 涂层柔韧试验不开裂、不剥落；② 弯后再镀：避免镀层弯裂，但弯后须重新表面处理 + 补镀。
  • 几何要求：角度 ≤90°、公差 ±2°、最小弯曲半径 R 与直段 Ls 见 Table 5.2（规格16 R102/Ls38；27 R146/Ls48；103 R406/Ls86）。📄 UL797 §5.4/Tbl5.2·p15
  • 风险点：弯曲外侧镀层开裂、焊缝在弯曲中张开、截面椭圆变形——本质仍依赖焊后退火 + 镀层附着这两关。
三个最致命阶段（按踩坑概率，控住这三步认证成功率大幅提升）： ① ⑦表面前处理不彻底 → 涂层附着失败（硫酸铜/盐雾）； ② ④焊后退火漏做 → 低温弯曲焊缝脆裂； ③ ⑧镀锌焊缝区偏薄 → 硫酸铜露铁。

④ 现状盘点 市场 vs 我方

🌐 市场现状
  • 宝来 / 伊莱克曼等竞品均为外采预镀锌管，无自有 UL 认证。
  • EMT 是北美刚需(电气线管强制品类)，需求稳定。
  • 规格区间 1/2″ – 4″；常用 3/4″、1″、2″。
  • 长度统一 10 ft(北美标准件)。
  • 客户现场手弯安装，对「易弯、不开裂」是硬性偏好。
🏭 我方进展
  • 工艺已判定可行。
  • 已锁定无锡 ERW 焊管厂；基材 SPCC(与内枪管同料)；光亮焊管去焊缝后再镀。
  • 自有250–300g 吹镀路线已成型。
  • 壁厚精度已能做到 ±0.1mm(优于焊管常规 ±5%)。
  • 认证工作内部已有专人负责，初判流程不复杂。
  • 无锡焊管厂报价、供应商样品枪打测厚(Task 106031 / 106032)推进中。
1/2–4″
规格范围
10 ft
统一长度
250 g/m²
目标镀层(吹镀)
±0.1mm
壁厚精度

⑤ 关键差距 / 待决策 核心 · 决策矩阵

工艺已通，剩下的全是「选哪条路」的决策问题。下表 6 项决定认证可行性 + 周期 + 成本，按优先级排列。

议题现状 / 选项缺口影响
1. 镀锌路线
最高优先		 预镀锌 5–10μm ✕ 过不了硫酸铜
250g 吹镀 ✓ 推荐
锌铝镁 ZAM ⚠ 表面好 + 能过硫酸铜		 ZAM 无已知 UL 797 先例，需机构评估；预镀锌锌层太薄已被排除。 直接决定认证可行性与周期，是全盘第一卡点。
2. 单厂 vs 双厂 吹镀=焊管厂内自做(单厂)
热镀锌=焊管厂 + 镀锌厂(双厂)		 即「成品认证 vs 厂家认证」之辨；UL 体系无独立厂家认证，工厂地址绑定 E 号。 双厂 = 多一次初检 + 多一次年度 FUS，成本与周期翻倍。
3. ZAM 化学成分合规 需确认 CSA C22.2 No.83.1 / UL 797 对替代涂层有无化学成分要求。 标准对替代涂层按性能(§6.2.4 盐雾 / CO₂-SO₂ / UV)而非成分判定，但需机构工程预评估背书。 若机构不接受替代涂层路径，ZAM 方案直接作废。
4. 手弯性能 客户手弯 ≥ 90°(宣传册指标)。 需通过 §6.1 常温 + 低温(0°C)弯曲试验、焊缝不开裂；ZAM 卷成管的手弯性能仍需实测。 弯裂 = 客户拒收 + 认证失败双重风险。
5. 基材牌号 SPCC / Q195 低碳钢 ✓ 方向正确 必须低碳无 Cr/Mo；含铬合金钢会镀锌失效，不可用。 选错基材 → 镀层附着失败、ERW 焊接困难，整线返工。
📐 为什么吹镀过硫酸铜、预镀锌过不了？(技术注解)
硫酸铜浸渍(CuSO₄)试验通过锌层被溶穿后铜析出判定锌层连续性与厚度。预镀锌带钢锌层仅 5–10μm(约 35–70 g/m² 单面)，在切口、焊缝处更薄，硫酸铜数次浸渍即露铁判废。自有吹镀做到 250 g/m²(约 35μm/单面)有充足余量。ZAM(锌铝镁)虽锌当量低，但表面致密氧化膜延缓溶解，实测可过——这是 ZAM 方案的主要卖点，但它属替代涂层，须机构按性能路径单独认可。 详见 🧪 测试要求 与 📋 适用标准。

⑥ 达成路线图 6 步

1
锁定基材 + 镀锌工艺
基材定 低碳钢 SPCC / Q195(参见 🔩 钢材选型)；镀层优先 250g 吹镀；ZAM 不可单方面拍板，须先做机构预咨询(Step 3)再决定是否纳入。
2
确定单厂 / 双厂结构
尽量找焊管 + 吹镀一体厂(或同址增设吹镀线)，把认证范围压到单一工厂地址，规避双厂多一次初检 + 多一次 FUS 的成本。详见 🏭 工厂检查。
3
联系认证机构做工程预评估 关键前置
对接 UL Solutions 广州 / 上海(见 🏢 认证机构)，一次性问清三件事：① ZAM 是否走替代涂层路径、② 有无化学成分硬要求、③ UL 排期与样品要求。投钱打样前必须先过这关。
4
打样与内部自测(并行)
  • 内部硫酸铜自测(自购设备，前置以避免实验室返工)
  • 供应商样品枪打测厚 — Task 106031
  • 无锡焊管厂报价 — Task 106032
  • 制备常温 / 低温弯曲试样(§6.1)
5
正式申请 → 测试 → 初检 → 拿 E 号
方案锁定后正式提交 → 实验室 UL 797 全套测试(测试要求) → 工厂初检(工厂检查) → 取得 cULus E 号。流程全貌见 📝 申请流程。
6
季度 FUS 维护 + 变更管理
拿证非终点：维持季度 FUS(跟踪服务检查)，任何材料 / 工艺 / 厂址变更须申报变更，否则 E 号失效。费用与周期见 💰 费用周期。

⑦ 我的建议 基于标准与资料搜集

以下为综合 UL 797 / CSA C22.2 No.83.1 标准条文、ASTM/JIS 钢材数据与认证流程资料后的独立判断，非频道原话转述。

⚠ 最大风险 — 镀锌路线摇摆
在「250g 吹镀」与「ZAM」之间反复，尤其 ZAM 无 UL 797 先例，机构评估 + 替代涂层论证(§6.2.4 盐雾 / CO₂-SO₂ / UV)可能拖 3–6 个月，是当前最大的进度黑洞。
✓ 推荐主线 — 250g 吹镀 · 单厂 · SPCC 基材
锌层余量足、过硫酸铜稳、单一厂址把认证范围压到最小。以此为基准方案推进认证，ZAM 作为「拿证后再增列的优化项」并行评估，不阻塞主线。
💡 五条落地建议
  1. 基材锁定 SPCC：低碳、无 Cr/Mo、镀锌相容好、延性高(易手弯)，与 EMT 基材标准高度吻合。唯一注意：SPCC 属商业级、强度偏软(典型抗拉 270–340 MPa)，略低于 A1011 SS33 的 360 MPa 目标——但 UL 797 本就不设强度门槛(由弯曲+尺寸把关)，必要时改半硬态 / 略高碳热批次即可补强。详见 🔩 钢材选型。
  2. 预咨询前置：投钱打样前先做一次机构免费 / 低成本预咨询，一次问清 ZAM 替代涂层路径、化学成分要求、UL 排期。
  3. 自测前置：把硫酸铜内部自测放到送实验室之前，避免外部测试返工(返工 = 钱 + 周期双输)。
  4. 单厂优先：尽量找焊管 + 吹镀一体厂，规避双厂多一次初检 + 多一次年度 FUS。
  5. 主线锁定：对外报价与认证主线统一锁 250g 吹镀，ZAM 仅作平行验证，避免供应侧单点推动改变既定路线。
← 返回总览 · 知识库首页

🏢 认证执行

认证机构 · 申请流程 · 费用与周期 · 工厂检查 —— UL Solutions（cULus）单一路线全流程

本页路线：PrecisePipe 走 UL Solutions（cULus）单一认证路线，一次覆盖美国（UL 797 / NEC）+ 加拿大（CSA C22.2 No.83.1 / CEC）。下面按 🏢 认证机构 · 📝 申请流程 · 💰 费用与周期 · 🏭 工厂检查 四段给出完整执行细节。关键路径是实验室测试（UL 797 全套），最常见失败是焊缝区锌层烧损导致 Preece 铜沉积——内部 Preece 自检前置可大幅降低返工风险。

🏢 认证机构 UL Solutions

主路径：UL Solutions（cULus） — 通过 UL Solutions 申请，获得 cULus 标志，同时覆盖美国（NEC）和加拿大（CEC）市场。这是 cUL 认证的标准路径，认证文件有 E 号，可在 UL Product iQ 数据库公开查询。
🔵
UL Solutions — UL Listed / cULus

品牌认知最高 cULus 双覆盖 费用略高

总体时间线（无返工最佳情况）

■ 准备阶段 ■ 行政阶段 ■ 测试阶段（高风险） ■ 完成/维护
1
锁定产品规格（产品定义）
2–4 周

在提交申请前，所有产品参数必须确定并冻结。认证期间任何变更（材料、镀层、供应商）都需重新评估，可能导致重新测试。

需要确认的决策点
贸易规格号段（例：1/2" 至 4"，是否一次申请全部还是分批）
镀锌工艺：吹镀250g/m²（推荐）vs 预镀锌 vs 热镀锌
是否使用锌铝镁（ZAM）镀层（⚠ 需先向认证机构预咨询）
申请范围：美国单市场（UL Listed）还是美+加（cULus）
焊管厂和镀锌厂是否为同一家工厂（影响工厂检查范围）
钢材牌号确认（SPCC / Q195，Grade B）
2
选择认证机构并预联系
1 周

选择认证机构：UL Solutions（广州/上海实验室，联系方式见机构页）。

  • 发邮件/电话说明：产品（EMT 钢制电线管）、申请标准（UL 797 + cULus）、规格范围、镀锌工艺
  • 询问：是否有 ZAM 镀层的工程预评估经验；当前排期
  • 请对方提供正式报价（Quote）
3
提交申请 + 准备文件包
1–3 周（机构审查）
申请文件清单
工程图纸：各规格 OD、壁厚、公差、长度（含角接箱接口尺寸）
物料清单（BOM）：钢材牌号、镀锌类型、镀层重量、联接件规格
制造工艺描述：ERW成管 → 焊缝处理 → 镀锌（工艺流程图）
QC 计划：在线检测方法、抽样频次、不合格品处理流程
工厂信息：工厂名称、地址（含镀锌外协厂地址）、组织架构
检测设备清单：游标卡尺、千分尺、硫酸铜测试设备等
公司营业执照（中英文）

机构审查申请后出具正式报价，包含测试范围、费用明细、时间表。在支付前确认所有规格都在报价范围内。

4
支付首期费用 + 寄送样品
2–3 周
  • 支付申请首期费用（通常为总费用 50–70%）
  • 样品数量：每个规格 2–5 支，申请 1/2"–4" 全系列约需 30–50 支
  • 推荐分批寄送（先寄 1/2"、3/4"、1" 三个最常用规格）
  • UL 广州实验室可直接国内快递，避免跨国报关
⚠ 注意：样品必须是正式生产批次，不接受手工制作的样件。样品的材料、工艺、镀层必须与量产完全一致。
5
实验室测试
4–8 周（⚠ 最高风险阶段）

实验室按 UL 797 + ANSI C80.3 执行全套测试（详见测试要求页）：

  • 硫酸铜（Preece）测试 — 最常见失败点
  • 锌层重量检测（ASTM A90）
  • 外径、壁厚、长度、直线度测量
  • 90° 弯管测试
  • 涂层柔韧性、低温测试
  • 标记完整性验证
⚠ 测试失败影响：任一项测试失败 → 需改进产品/工艺 → 重新提交样品 → 重新测试，额外增加 6–10 周 + 原测试费 50–75%。

最常见失败原因：焊缝区域锌层烧损（吹镀时焊缝处理不足）→ 铜硫酸盐测试在焊缝位置出现铜沉积。

6
工厂初检
2–4 周

认证机构派工程师到中国工厂现场验证生产条件。UL Solutions 在广州/深圳/上海有本地工程师，可快速安排。

  • 验证：实际生产工艺与申请文件一致
  • 检查：QC 设备校准记录、来料检验、在线检测
  • 如涉及两个工厂（焊管厂 + 镀锌厂），两处均需检查
  • 工厂必须具备执行 Preece 铜硫酸盐测试的设备和操作规程

初检时长：通常 4 小时至 1 天。如有不符合项，给予整改期，期满复查。

7
获颁证书 / 文件号（E 号）
1–2 周

产品测试和工厂初检均通过后，认证机构颁发：

  • 认证报告（Certification Report / File） — 载明产品规格、工厂地址、认证范围
  • E 号（UL） — 在 UL Product iQ 数据库公开可查
  • UL 标志使用授权 — 可在产品上连续印制认证标志
8
持续维护：季度 FUS 跟踪检查
每年 4 次，持续

认证不是一次性的。获证后，认证机构每季度（约每 90 天）对工厂进行不事先通知的跟踪检查（Follow-Up Services / FUS）。

  • 从成品库中抽取样品，现场测量尺寸、检查标记
  • 审查上次检查后的生产记录和 QC 记录
  • 验证认证标志使用的合规性
  • 任何工艺变更（新供应商、镀锌工艺调整）必须提前报告并获批

FUS 年度费用：约 $4,200–$8,000（含季检 + 文件维护 + 标志授权），详见费用页。

⚠ 容易忽视的重要规则

场景规则
更换钢材供应商必须提前通知认证机构，可能需重新测试
调整镀锌工艺参数必须提前报告，视变更程度决定是否重测
增加新规格（如5"）需补充申请，新规格须单独测试
增加新工厂/车间新厂必须单独做工厂初检，获批后才能生产认证产品
在非认证工厂生产严重违规，可导致认证暂停或吊销
未经授权使用UL标志法律责任，UL会追究
初次认证费用（UL Solutions，cULus 主路径）
标准 cUL 认证路径 · 获 cULus 标志 · 覆盖美国+加拿大
申请/工程审查费$1,400–$4,000
产品测试费（UL 797 全套）$5,000–$15,000
工厂初检（中国，含差旅）$1,500–$3,000
认证文件费已含
初次认证合计$8,000–$22,000
年度维护费用（UL Solutions）
获证后每年持续费用
4次季度 FUS 检查（中国工厂）$3,000–$5,000
年度文件维护费（最低）~¥8,500 (~$1,170)
UL 标志使用授权费$1,000–$3,000/年
年度维护合计$5,170–$9,170/年

UL Solutions — 最佳情况时间线（无返工）

W0
W2
W4
W6
W8
W10
W12
W14
W16
W18
W20
W24
产品定义
2–4周
申请+报价
4周
文件审查
2周
寄样品
2周
实验室测试
6–8周
工厂初检
4周
证书颁发
2周
总计：约 18–26周（4.5–6.5个月）

⚠ 一次测试失败场景（最常见：Preece 测试失败）

额外费用
重新测试费（原测试费 × 50–75%）+$2,500–$7,500
工艺整改（镀锌线调整）+$1,000–$5,000
新样品制作+运输+$500–$1,500
额外总费用+$4,000–$14,000
额外时间
工艺整改时间+2–4周
新样品生产+运输+2–3周
重新测试排期+4–6周
额外总时间+8–13周

含一次返工总费用： $12,000–$36,000 | 总周期： 24–35周（6–9个月）

避免返工的关键措施：
  • 先做内部 Preece 测试（自购测试设备，约 $200–$500）验证样品
  • 委托第三方检测机构（SGS、TÜV）在送 NRTL 前预检
  • 向镀锌厂明确 250g/m² 均匀覆盖要求，特别关注焊缝处理

ZAM 镀层方案额外成本估算

ZAM 无已知 UL 797 先例，需走替代涂层认证路径，额外测试和工程审查费用显著。

额外项目估算费用额外时间
认证机构工程预评估$1,000–$3,0002–4周
600小时盐雾试验（ASTM B117）$2,000–$5,00015–20周
CO₂/SO₂ 腐蚀试验$1,500–$3,0003–6周
UV/水试验$1,000–$2,0002–4周
ZAM 弯曲性能专项测试$500–$1,5002–3周
ZAM 额外总费用+$6,000–$14,500+3–6个月
总体风险：如果认证机构在工程评估阶段要求额外数据，或拒绝 ZAM 方案，前期投入的工程咨询费和等待时间可能完全浪费。强烈建议先做免费/低成本的预咨询，再决定是否投入。

5年总拥有成本（TCO）对比

费用项目 UL Solutions（cULus） 含一次返工
初次认证$8,000–$22,000$12,000–$36,000
年度维护 × 5年$25,850–$45,850$25,850–$45,850
5年总计 $33,850–$67,850 $37,850–$81,850
5年平均每年$6,770–$13,570$7,570–$16,370
注：含一次返工列在 UL 基准上叠加返工附加成本（+$4,000–$14,000）。以上为我方估算，实际以认证机构正式报价为准。
项目中提出的核心问题："做认证是成品认证还是厂家认证？如果要热镀锌，就涉及焊管厂 + 镀锌厂 2 个厂。" — 本页专门解答这个问题。

成品认证 vs 厂家认证：UL 体系的真实结构

📄 UL 认证的实际结构

在 UL 体系中，没有独立的"工厂认证"。cULus 认证结构是：

  • E 号 / 文件号 = 特定产品 + 特定标准 + 特定工厂的三方绑定认证
  • 产品必须通过测试；工厂必须通过初检和持续 FUS
  • 换工厂生产 → 新工厂须重新做工厂初检，加入文件

所谓"成品认证"，就是指产品测试通过并拿到 E 号。UL 认证 = 产品测试 + 工厂绑定，两者不可分离。

🏭 cULus 认证的"厂家"含义

UL 体系中没有独立的"厂家认证"。"认证了工厂"的真实含义是：

  • 该工厂地址被写入 UL 认证文件（E 号），并通过了 UL 初检
  • 该工厂每季度接受 UL Follow-Up Services 跟踪审核
  • 工厂有权生产带 cULus 标志的该 E 号产品

更换工厂地址或新增工厂，须重新做初检并更新文件，才能继续生产认证产品。

多工厂场景：焊管厂 + 镀锌厂联合申报

场景 A：焊管厂和镀锌厂是同一家工厂（最简单）

无锡焊管厂 A
ERW 焊管 → 吹镀 250g
→
认证产品
UL 797 Listed
仅需对一家工厂做初检和后续 FUS。认证文件列一个工厂地址。最低成本、最快路径。

场景 B：焊管厂（无锡）+ 外协镀锌厂（友发/其他）—— 项目讨论的实际方案

无锡焊管厂
ERW 成管 + 去焊缝
→
友发 / 镀锌厂
250g/m² 吹镀
→
认证产品
UL 797 Listed
认证机构需要检查两个工厂：
  • 焊管厂：验证成管工艺、尺寸控制、来料检验
  • 镀锌厂：验证镀锌线参数、锌层重量控制、Preece 测试能力
  • 两个工厂地址均列入认证文件，均需年度 FUS 检查
  • 额外费用：多一次工厂初检 + 每年多一次 FUS（约多 $1,500–$3,000/年）
建议：尽量找同时有焊管和镀锌能力的单一工厂，简化认证范围。如果两厂分开，需要在申请阶段就明确告知认证机构两厂地址。

工厂初检（Initial Factory Inspection）详情

检查形式
  • 认证机构派工程师到工厂现场
  • 时长：4 小时至 1 天（视工厂规模）
  • UL Solutions 在广州、深圳、上海均有本地工程师
检查结果
  • ✓ 通过：工厂加入认证文件，授权生产
  • ⚠ 有条件通过：整改期内完成，复查
  • ✗ 不通过：整改后重新安排检查（+额外费用）

初检核查清单

生产设备与工艺
ERW 成管线设备清单和参数记录
焊缝处理工序（去焊缝/光亮焊管）
镀锌线参数（锌温、锌液成分、吹镀风刀设置）
焊缝区域镀锌均匀性保障措施
QC 检测设备
外径测量设备（游标卡尺/激光测径仪）及校准记录
壁厚测量设备（千分尺）及校准记录
铜硫酸盐（Preece）测试设备和操作规程
锌层重量检测能力（磁性测厚仪或重量法）
长度测量工具
记录与体系
来料钢卷检验记录（牌号、批次、镀层重量）
在线抽检频次和记录格式
不合格品隔离和处理流程
标记（打标/印字）系统验证
成品储存和标识（可追溯批次）

Follow-Up Services（FUS）季度跟踪检查

检查特点
  • 频率：每年 4 次（约每 90 天一次）
  • 方式：UL Solutions — 不提前通知（Unannounced）
  • 时长：2–4 小时/次
  • 范围：上次检查后的生产记录、库存抽样
FUS 检查内容
  • 从成品库抽取样品，现场测量 OD、壁厚
  • 目视检查 UL 标志印制是否合规
  • 审查 Preece 测试在线记录
  • 确认生产工艺无未经申报的变更
  • 检查 QC 设备是否已校准
FUS 发现问题后的处理流程
问题严重程度认证机构动作工厂要求
轻微不符开具整改通知（Variation Notice）规定期限内整改，书面回复
重大不符暂停认证标志使用权整改后复查，通过才能恢复
工艺重大变更未申报立即暂停，可能吊销认证补充申报 + 可能重测 + 罚款
未经授权使用UL标志法律追究（UL独立追查）严重法律风险

变更管理：什么变了必须申报

变更类型是否需申报是否可能触发重测建议操作
换钢卷供应商（同牌号）是视情况提前书面告知，等待批准
调整镀锌参数（锌层量）是（重大变更）很可能提前申报，准备重测
切换镀锌工厂是（重大变更）是，新厂需初检新厂初检通过前不得生产
增加新规格（如3/4" → 增加2-1/2"）是新规格需测试单独申请追加规格
更换 ERW 成管工艺（设备）是视情况评估后决定
更换钢材牌号（如 SPCC → Q195B）是（重大）很可能提前申报，提供材料规格证明
增加新生产厂区/分厂是（重大）新厂初检未通过初检不得生产
更改产品标记格式是不需要提交新标记格式审批

工厂现在可以开始准备的事项

采购铜硫酸盐测试设备——可在线购买基础设备套件（约 ¥1,500–5,000），建立在线自检能力
建立 QC 台账——每班次记录外径、壁厚抽检值，为 FUS 做准备
校准所有测量设备——确保游标卡尺、千分尺有有效校准证书
确认焊缝处镀锌工艺——与镀锌厂沟通焊缝区域特殊处理要求
整理来料检验记录——钢卷的入厂检验、批次记录、镀层证书
起草工艺描述文件（中英文）——成管工艺 + 镀锌工艺流程图
确定标记方案——连续印字系统（喷码/滚印），确认标记内容和间距符合 UL 797 §9
总结：PrecisePipe 建议的推进路径
  1. CA 团队联系 UL Solutions 广州/上海做预评估咨询
  2. 明确焊管厂和镀锌厂是否分开（决定检查范围和费用）
  3. 如考虑 ZAM，请认证机构工程师给出化学成分/替代涂层路径意见
  4. 向无锡焊管厂询价时同时确认对方是否有配套镀锌能力
  5. 锁定方案后提交正式申请，进入测试流程
下一章：我方策略 →
